Code fragment: public final TableField<PostRecord, Integer> ID = createField("id", org.jooq.impl.SQLDataType.INTEGER.nullable(false).defaultValue(org.jooq.impl.DSL.field("nextval('post_id_seq'::regclass)", org.jooq.impl.SQLDataType.INTEGER)), this, "");
Explanation: The primary key column is named just 'id', which is a generic name that doesn't convey any meaning about the table it belongs to. Consider renaming it to 'post_id' to make it more descriptive and to avoid ambiguity in queries with joins.

Code fragment: public final TableField<UserRecord, Integer> ID = createField("id", org.jooq.impl.SQLDataType.INTEGER.nullable(false).defaultValue(org.jooq.impl.DSL.field("nextval('user_id_seq'::regclass)", org.jooq.impl.SQLDataType.INTEGER)), this, "");
Explanation: The primary key is named just 'id', which is a generic name that doesn't convey any meaning about the table it belongs to. Consider renaming it to 'user_id' to make it more descriptive and to avoid ambiguity in queries with joins.

Using `select()` without specifying columns fetches all columns from both USER and POST tables, which wastes bandwidth and memory. Explicitly list only the needed columns: `select(POST.TITLE, USER.FIRST_NAME, USER.LAST_NAME)`.
